prompt:
Nightly we dump the product catalog (sorted by key) and diff it against what the search index says it has (also sorted by key) to work out what to upsert and what to delete. The current script loads both dumps into maps — 6GB of RSS for what is fundamentally a merge walk over two sorted lists. Rewrite the core as a proper reconciler I can reuse.

Package reconcile, root of module go-streamdiff, stdlib only:

  type Pair struct { Key, Value string }
  type Op int
  const ( OpAdded Op = iota; OpRemoved; OpChanged )
  type Change struct { Op Op; Key string; Old, New string }
  func Reconcile(before, after []Pair, batchSize int, flush func([]Change) error) error
  var ErrUnsorted, ErrDuplicateKey

Contract:
- Both inputs claim to be strictly ascending by Key. Walk them in ONE pass, merge-style, emitting changes in ascending key order: key only in after -> OpAdded (Old empty), key only in before -> OpRemoved (New empty), key in both with different values -> OpChanged carrying both, identical values -> silence.
- Deliveries are batched: hand flush exactly batchSize changes per call, remainder in a final smaller call. Never call flush with an empty batch. Each batch must be a slice flush can KEEP — the downstream writer holds them until commit, so no recycling one buffer (that bug is literally why the index has doorstop entries today). If flush returns an error, stop right there and return that error; no more flush calls.
- batchSize must be >= 1, otherwise error out before doing anything.
- Don't trust the inputs: a key <= its predecessor within a sequence means the dump is broken. Equal consecutive keys -> ErrDuplicateKey; decreasing -> ErrUnsorted. Because this must stay single-pass over huge dumps, validate AS you walk: full batches emitted before the bad element are already flushed and stay flushed, the in-progress partial batch is dropped, and the error comes back.

reconcile_test.go is the acceptance suite — the batching/aliasing and mid-stream-failure cases are exactly the bugs I'm trying to bury, so don't touch it. Passing go test -race -timeout 30s ./... is the bar.
